notes: Sailor Huey (trainer) at tile(9,3) facing right — line of sight 3, triggers battle
Gentleman Alfred (trainer) at tile(17,8) facing left — line of sight 3, triggers battle
West staircase at (3,11) leads down to 1F warp 3
Center staircase at (5,3) leads up to 3F warp 2
East staircases at (16,11)/(17,11) lead up to 3F warps 4-5
East staircases at (16,13)/(17,13) lead down to 1F warps 4-5
Floor has holes/gaps that prevent direct east-west traversal — must navigate around them
Sailor Huey is a phone-registered rematch trainer with 4 escalating teams

trainers.name: Huey
trainers.rematch_note: Huey registers his phone number after battle. He calls for rematches with escalating teams: HUEY2 (Lv28 Poliwhirl x2) after Radio Tower cleared; HUEY3 (Lv34 Poliwhirl + Poliwrath) after Elite Four; HUEY4 (Lv38 Politoed + Poliwrath with moves) after Kanto restored. Beating HUEY4 can earn a Protein.
trainers.seen_text: Men of the sea are always spoiling for a good fight!
trainers.after_text: Men of the sea are always spoiling for a good fight!

trainers.name: Alfred
trainers.seen_text: Hm? This is no place for playing.
trainers.beaten_text: Ah! I can see that you're serious.
trainers.after_text: Up top is a Pokemon that keeps the LIGHTHOUSE lit. But I hear that it's sick now and can't be cured by ordinary medicine.

notes: Second floor of the Olivine Lighthouse. Has two trainers: Sailor Huey (registerable rematch trainer with 4 escalating teams keyed to story progress) and Gentleman Alfred (single battle, lore about the sick Pokemon on top). The floor has multiple staircases — west stairs at (3,11) go back to 1F, center stairs at (5,3) go to 3F, and east side has two pairs of stairs at (16-17,11) going to 3F and (16-17,13) going to 1F. The split east staircases reflect the floor's hole-heavy layout requiring navigation around gaps. Gentleman Alfred's after-battle dialogue is the first in-game hint about Amphy's sickness and the need for special medicine.
